bindings.quest_action: arm the militia and prepare defenses against a reported enemy attack
bindings.quest_focus: a scout report claiming enemy forces are massing nearby
bindings.quest_stakes: the settlement will be overrun if the attack comes while unprepared
bindings.quest_threat: a potential invasion force that demands immediate response
bindings.quest_time_pressure: every hour of delay is another hour for the enemy to surround us

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I don't have time to explain everything properly. We need to act now and ask questions later.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} from {location_hint}, and {target_npc} confirmed seeing tracks near {location}. The council has authorized a preemptive strike. I need {quest_item} to {quest_action}. {quest_time_pressure}: {quest_threat} won't wait for us to debate.
dialogueChunks.outro: Speed matters more than caution right now. {quest_stakes} if we don't move.

playerResponses.text: I'll gather what you need. Better prepared than sorry.
Has anyone actually confirmed hostile intent? Never mind, I'll help.
Acting on a single scout report sounds reckless. I'll pass.

bindings.quest_action: follow the next shipment to catch the suspected traitor in the act
bindings.quest_focus: three missing shipments all handled by the same person
bindings.quest_stakes: vital supplies keep vanishing and the settlement is running short
bindings.quest_threat: an insider tipping off raiders in exchange for safe passage
bindings.quest_origin: years of trust that make the betrayal pattern unmistakable

dialogueChunks.intro: Listen carefully, {player_name}. We've been betrayed, and I know exactly who did it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} on the road from {location} in the past month. Every one handled by {target_npc}. That's not coincidence: that's {quest_threat}. The route passes through {enemy_type} territory {location_hint}. I need you to {quest_action} and recover whatever stolen goods you can find.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_origin} is what makes this so clear. They're the only one who knew the schedule.

playerResponses.text: If they're guilty, I'll find the proof. I'll follow the route.
Three shipments is suspicious. But people aren't always what they seem.
Accusations without hard evidence lead to injustice. Sort it out properly.

bindings.quest_action: investigate a traveler's accusation before mob justice takes over
bindings.quest_focus: a single traveler's claim that a resident was meeting with raiders
bindings.quest_stakes: an innocent person is locked in the storehouse based on hearsay
bindings.quest_threat: a settlement too angry to wait for evidence before passing judgment
bindings.quest_origin: a stranger's accusation that arrived without proof and left without answers

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need someone with a clear head. Everyone here is too angry to think straight, including me.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A traveler accused {target_npc} of meeting with a raider boss {location_hint}. The settlement is in uproar. {quest_stakes}: we locked them up while we decide what to do. But {quest_origin}: the traveler left before anyone could question them. I need you to {quest_action}. Follow the trail, find evidence, and gather {quest_item} that was allegedly part of the deal.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_threat} is fast and wrong. Real justice takes time and truth. Help me find both.

playerResponses.text: Truth first, judgment after. I'll find what happened.
Locking someone up on a stranger's word? This smells wrong. I'll investigate.
Your settlement, your problem. I'm not playing judge.
